item.title: Molecular Dialogues in the Mitochondria-Microbiome Crosstalk: Metabolites, Signaling, and Immunity.

item.title: The gut microbiome: Recent findings and future opportunities in cystic fibrosis.
item.description: Journal of cystic fibrosis : official journal of the European Cystic Fibrosis Society - Review - The gut microbiome is a key modulator of human health throughout life, from infancy to old age. Within this relatively young field, microbiome characteristics are being utilized as useful clinical ...

item.title: Clinical combination therapy for IBD and promising co-delivery strategies.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology - Review - Biological agents are the cornerstone of first-line treatment for inflammatory bowel disease. However, approximately 33% of patients either fail to respond initially or experience diminished effica...

item.description: Biological trace element research - Ulcerative colitis (UC) constitutes a principal subtype of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and exhibits escalating global incidence. This study elucidates the therapeutic efficacy and mechanistic ...

item.title: Ovotransferrin Alleviates Ulcerative Colitis by Modulating Ligilactobacillus murinus-Mediated Bile Acid Metabolism via the Ptgs2/JAK-STAT Signaling Axis.
item.description: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry - Ovotransferrin (OVT) is a functionally active protein demonstrated to alleviate ulcerative colitis (UC), although its mechanism remains incompletely understood. Notably, UC is closely associated wi...

item.title: OTOP2 Proton Channel Couples Luminal pH Sensing to Intestinal Immune Homeostasis.

item.title: The Assessment of Multidimensional Clinical, Biological and Patient-Reported Outcomes to Evaluate the Efficacy of Add-On Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG Supplementation in Mild Ulcerative Colitis: A Randomized Pilot Trial.
